summary: Micro-Computed Tomography (micro-CT) cannot reliably assess the true microstructure—such as trabecular architecture or cell-level details—of the maxillary alveolar bone because it does not provide sufficient resolution for these fine-scale characteristics; instead, only macroscopic morphological assessments are possible with this technology. Histology remains essential for accurate quantification of actual microstructures due to its superior detail at a subcellular level compared to all forms of computed radiographic techniques like CBCT.

Histology remains essential for accurate quantification of actual microstructures due to its superior detail at a subcellular level compared to all forms of computed radiographic techniques like CBCT.. Answer choice: B
